# Test Automation Summary — Story 6.6

**Story:** Final Contrast, Typography, and Provenance-Label Polish
**Stage:** Combined QA + Code Review (defense-tier additions layered on top of substantive dev)
**Date:** 2026-05-25
**Generated by:** `bmad-code-review` (combined QA + CR pass)

---

## Scope handed to the combined QA + CR stage

Story 6.6 is the launch-week visual polish + a11y final sweep — predominantly **documentation-heavy** (new contrast audit at `docs/accessibility/contrast-audit-launch-week.md` and reference-parity review template at `docs/launch/reference-parity-review.md`), with a focused code-side delta:

- **AC1 contrast fixes** — 4 sub-18px non-HUD sites switched from `--v-color-fg-quiet` (3.20:1) to `--v-color-fg-muted` (7.32:1).
- **AC6 L4 HUD-region masking + tolerance tightening** — `mask: [v-hud, v-chapter-copy, v-timeline-scrubber]` on every L4 screenshot capture; `maxDiffPixelRatio` 0.005 → 0.001 (5× tightening; restores Story 4.9 AC2 original target).
- **AC7 token-ification** — `--v-size-shortcut-key-col: 100px` added to `tokens.css`; `v-help-overlay.ts .shortcut-keys` consumes it; deferred-work `[2.8/LOW]` entry struck through.
- **AC2 / AC5 vitest + L4 additions** — `tabular-numerals-invariance.test.ts` (6 tests) + `focus-persistence.spec.ts` (2 Playwright tests).

QA's focused scope (per the parent orchestrator prompt): doc-integrity defense tests (≥30 assertions) covering audit doc structure, reference-parity template completeness, AC7 token correctness, AC6 masking + tolerance, AC1 contrast token switches, AC2 tabular-nums declarations, closing pointers (deferred-work strike-through, Epic 5 retro Action item #3 pointer), bright-backdrop manual-checklist cross-reference, and Story 6.5 findings unpopulated defer note.

Adversarial review focused on AC6 masking correctness (clips the right surfaces, not just `<v-hud>` host) and AC8 template completeness.

### Playwright L4 visual + L4 a11y suites

NOT re-run by the QA+CR stage. Per AC9 the **lead's smoke step (b)** runs `npm run build && npx playwright test --config tests/visual/playwright.config.ts --update-snapshots` to re-capture all 17 baselines under the new `mask: [...]` parameters + tightened 0.001 tolerance, then validates determinism across 3 consecutive reruns. The dev agent and QA stage cannot execute this step (no build-then-screenshot pipeline available to sub-agents per Rule 7).

Lead's smoke-evidence README documents the 6-step verification: `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/6-6-smoke-evidence/README.md`. Determinism check + baseline re-capture are routed there.

### Chrome DevTools MCP smoke stage (per skill-rules.md Rule 3 + Rule 8)

Story 6.6 touches `web/src/` (4 contrast fixes + AC7 token consumption) but the user-facing surface verification routes through:

1. **AC9 lead-smoke step (a)** — `cd web && npm test` ≥ 3812 passing + new tests (verified at 3875 post-QA).
2. **AC9 lead-smoke step (b)** — Playwright L4 visual re-capture + 3-rerun determinism check (lead-driven).
3. **AC5 Playwright spec** — `web/tests/visual/focus-persistence.spec.ts` runs against the production build in Chromium; verifies the NFR-A3 / NFR-A4 contract at runtime in a real browser (ADR-0010 compliant).
4. **AC2 grep defense** — `tabular-numerals-invariance.test.ts` enumerates the 5 scrub-driven-digit components and asserts each declares `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums`.
5. **AC6 mask-locator defense** — `story-6-6-defense.test.ts` § AC6 asserts the mask declarations are present and target the three HUD-bearing custom elements.

Per Rule 3 the per-story smoke evidence is RUNTIME-binding via the lead's Playwright L4 step (b) + the AC5 focus-persistence spec running in real Chromium. The QA + CR stage's defense-tier additions are vitest-grep-tier and document the STATIC contract (source files match the audit doc claims).

| 2 | reference-parity-review.md template (AC8) | 13 | Template exists at AC8-canonical path, Status: TEMPLATE, § 1 Methodology, § 2 Reference set with 3 references + canonical URLs, § 3 Reviewer roster, § 5 Per-reviewer verdicts with verdict templates, § 6 Aggregate verdict TBD + PASS/BLOCKED decision rule, cross-refs to Story 6.5 + contrast audit |
| 3 | AC7 token-ification correctness | 4 | `--v-size-shortcut-key-col: 100px` in tokens.css, grouped under Component-specific size tokens, v-help-overlay.ts `.shortcut-keys` uses `var(...)`, NO remaining `100px` literal in the `.shortcut-keys` rule |
| 4 | AC1 contrast fixes | 5 | v-version.ts uses fg-muted (and not fg-quiet for the host text), v-help-overlay.ts `.toggle` uses fg-muted text + fg-quiet border, v-speed-multiplier.ts `.label` + `.readout` both fg-muted |
| 5 | AC6 L4 visual masking + tolerance tightening | 3 | playwright.config.ts maxDiffPixelRatio = 0.001 (and 0.005 is GONE), encounters.spec.ts has ≥ 2 mask blocks targeting v-hud / v-chapter-copy / v-timeline-scrubber, reduced-motion-regression.spec.ts has mask targeting same three locators |
| 6 | AC2 typography invariants | 6 | No decorative `<i>` in v-about-page (only `<em>`); tabular-nums declared on v-hud-date, v-hud-distance, v-hud-speed, v-hud-instruments, v-attitude-indicator |
| 7 | closing pointers + deferred-work cleanup | 3 | deferred-work.md `[2.8/LOW]` entry struck through with Story 6.6 closing pointer; Epic 5 retro Action item #3 closing pointer to Story 6.6; manual a11y checklist bright-backdrop cross-reference to audit doc |

| **Total** | | **55** | |

Note: the 9 defense blocks add up to 55 tests in the table; the file ships an additional 2 redundant assertions (an explicit "FAIL row count is 0" + the `existsSync` pre-check on contrast doc) for a total of **57 passing assertions**. All 57 pass on first run after a two-line markdown-bold-tolerance regex fix (the bolded `**WCAG version:**` and `**Status:**` headers required a `[*\s]+` separator instead of `\s+`).

---

## Adversarial review highlights

### AC6 masking correctness — verified safe (HIGH-priority adversarial focus)

The prompt flagged: "Verify the masking actually clips around HUD text (not just `<v-hud>` host element)."

**Finding: PASS.** The three locators target the three custom elements that wrap every variable-text region on the L4 visual baseline surface:

1. `v-hud` is the host of `v-hud-date`, `v-hud-distance`, `v-hud-speed`, `v-hud-instruments`, `v-hud-chapter-title`, AND `v-attitude-indicator` (imported and inline-rendered per `v-hud.ts:9,14`). Playwright's `mask: [Locator]` paints over the bounding rect of the located element — for a custom element with shadow DOM, this is the host's full layout rect, which includes ALL the inner HUD readouts. The HUD-text antialiasing flake that drove the 0.005 tolerance loose is fully covered.
2. `v-chapter-copy` is the chapter-copy serif-body panel — variable-text-bearing during PBD substate transitions.
3. `v-timeline-scrubber` is the L4 scrubber surface — chapter markers + thumb + per-chapter time labels.

**The remaining un-masked surface** is the canvas (deterministic per the Story 4.9 / 5.4 stable-frame contract) + the `v-version` light-DOM caption + the `v-help-overlay` toggle button (variable position, but text content is the static `?` glyph). The `v-version` token's textContent reads "VOYAGER 1.x.y" plus a build hash — IF the build hash drifts across reruns, that could surface as a sub-tolerance pixel-diff. Audit observation: this is acceptable risk because the Story 6.0 `--update-snapshot-discipline` doc requires re-capture under the production build pinned for the snapshot, NOT across drifting builds. The 3-rerun determinism check in the lead's smoke (step b) is the binding gate for this risk.

**Conclusion:** masking correctly clips around the canonical HUD-text-bearing surfaces. The 0.001 tightening is mathematically justified by the masking — antialiasing variance previously dominated the 0.005 budget, and it's now removed from the diff surface.

### AC7 token correctness — verified safe

The prompt flagged: "verify the literal `100px` is GONE from `v-help-overlay.ts`; verify the new token is referenced; cross-check the v-help-overlay tests don't pin the old literal."

**Finding: PASS.** Grep audit:

- `web/src/components/v-help-overlay.ts:206` — `min-width: var(--v-size-shortcut-key-col);` (token consumption).
- `web/src/components/v-help-overlay.ts` `.shortcut-keys` rule body — NO remaining `100px` literal (defense test § AC7.4 asserts this explicitly).
- `web/src/styles/tokens.css:80` — `--v-size-shortcut-key-col: 100px;` (under new "Component-specific size tokens" subsection).
- `web/src/components/v-help-overlay.test.ts` — no test pins the old literal `100px`. The only `100px` mention in the file is in narrative comments referring to the old state.

### AC8 template completeness — verified safe

The prompt flagged: "verify it's a complete template (methodology + reference URLs + reviewer roster + verdict format) with the verdict marked TBD."

**Finding: PASS.** All 7 sections present:

- § 1 Methodology — per-reviewer protocol + aggregate verdict criterion documented.
- § 2 Reference set — 3 references with canonical URLs (Apollo in Real Time: apolloinrealtime.org; NYT long-scroll: TBD with example URLs; FWA Three.js winner: thefwa.com).
- § 3 Reviewer roster — 1 maintainer + 2–3 external structure; recruitment mirrors Story 6.5.
- § 4 Session execution log — empty table awaiting maintainer.
- § 5 Per-reviewer verdicts — 4 reviewer slots (3 binding + 1 optional tie-break) with the binding verdict template ("Linkable without apology" / "Not yet").
- § 6 Aggregate verdict — marked **TO BE POPULATED AFTER EXTERNAL REVIEW**; PASS / BLOCKED decision rule documented at ≥ 2/3 binding.
- § 7 Cross-references — PRD, Story 6.5 friendly-user gate, contrast audit, Epic 7 Story 7.9, Rule 17.

### Story 6.5 friendly-user feedback defer note — verified safe

The prompt flagged: "if findings doc is unpopulated (LIKELY), Dev Agent Record should explicitly note this and defer follow-up. Verify the dev did this."

**Finding: PASS.** The story file explicitly defers in TWO places:

1. AC2 contract last clause acknowledges the conditional behaviour: `"if findings doc is unpopulated (likely), Story 6.6 explicitly notes this in the Dev Agent Record and defers to a follow-up..."`.
2. The contrast audit doc § 4.4 ("Story 6.5 friendly-user feedback on hierarchy") declares the unpopulated state explicitly: `"As of Story 6.6 audit time the document is the unpopulated template that Story 6.5 committed — TBD markers occupy every section."` and routes follow-up to a future story.

Defense test § Block 8 asserts this defer note exists in the story file.

## Findings — Triage Summary

| Severity | Resolved inline | Deferred | Dismissed |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| HIGH | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| MED | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| LOW | 0 | 0 | 0 |

**Zero net findings** across the three adversarial review focuses (AC6 masking, AC7 token correctness, AC8 template completeness) + Story 6.5 defer note + Rule 3/5/6/13 compliance audits. The dev stage's implementation matched the AC contract exactly; no auto-resolve patches, no deferrals, no dismissals.

### ADR compliance verified (Rule 6)

- **ADR-0010** (Playwright per AC6 — visual regression suite) — `mask:` parameters land in the L4 Playwright suite; tolerance tightened in `playwright.config.ts`. ADR-0010-compliant.
- **ADR-0025** (APG primitives — focus styling on slider + listbox + dialog) — Story 6.6 AC5 audit doc § 5.3 enumerates the three APG-primitive-consumer components (`<v-timeline-scrubber>` thumb + chapter-markers, `<v-speed-multiplier>` thumb, `<v-chapter-index>` options); each declares a per-component `:focus-visible` compensating style. ADR-0025-compliant.
- **ADR-0027** (LF line endings on new markdown) — the two new docs (`contrast-audit-launch-week.md`, `reference-parity-review.md`) plus the smoke-evidence README inherit `* text=auto eol=lf` from `.gitattributes`. ADR-0027-compliant.

### Rule audit (skill-rules.md)

- **Rule 3** (per-story smoke evidence) — Story 6.6's user-facing surface verified via the L4 + new focus-persistence Playwright spec (running in real Chromium against the production build). Lead-driven smoke step (b) re-captures baselines under the new mask + tolerance. Per Rule 7 (sub-agents inherit harness MCP) the lead is the binding gate for the build-then-screenshot pipeline.
- **Rule 5** (NFR tripwire response) — NOT TRIGGERED. None of AC1–AC10 surfaced an un-implementable NFR. AC1's contrast threshold + AC2's tabular-nums + AC5's focus-contract are all clearable in-place; the dev landed fixes inline.
- **Rule 6** (ADR violations are HIGH) — see ADR audit above; all three relevant ADRs verified compliant.
- **Rule 13** (test discoverability) — `web/tests/story-6-6-defense.test.ts` follows the project-canonical naming pattern; no `.skip`, no `.runIf`, no env-gate. The default `npm test` invocation picks it up (verified: 222 → 223 files, +57 tests, all passing).
- **Rule 16** (manual a11y checklist) — Story 6.6 touches user-facing surfaces (CSS contrast fixes); manual a11y checklist updated with the bright-backdrop cross-reference. Next milestone (Epic 6 close) re-runs the checklist per the policy.
- **Rule 17** (friendly-user launch gate) — NOT TRIGGERED at code review time (Rule 17 explicitly excludes code-review-time enforcement). Story 6.6's AC2 last clause + contrast audit § 4.4 defer the gate per the rule's protocol.

## Closing posture

Story 6.6 ships visual-polish + a11y launch-week readiness. The reference-parity verdict (AC8) is OUT-OF-BAND per the story's posture — the maintainer's follow-up commit populates the verdict in `docs/launch/reference-parity-review.md` § 6. The `committed` log entry from Story 6.6 signals visual-polish readiness, NOT launch-gate satisfaction.

The lead's smoke step (b) — `npm run build && npx playwright test --update-snapshots` + 3-rerun determinism — remains the binding gate for AC6's masking+tolerance contract going live. Defense tests pin the STATIC contract (config values, mask declarations, doc structure); the lead's runtime check pins the DYNAMIC contract (real Chromium baselines re-capture deterministically under the new tolerance).
